name: SPL S-Expression Parser
description: Tokenizer and recursive-descent parser for SPL S-expressions. Converts SPL source code strings into abstract syntax trees (AST) consisting of primitives (bool, int, float, str) and nested lists.

types.name: AST
types.description: Abstract syntax tree node - can be bool, int, float, str, or list[AST]

functions.name: tokenize
functions.description: Tokenizes an SPL S-expression source string into a list of string tokens. Handles parentheses, quoted strings, and whitespace-delimited symbols.
postconditions: Returns a list of tokens including '(', ')', quoted strings (with quotes), and symbols
String literals are preserved as single tokens with quotes intact
Whitespace is used to delimit non-string tokens

functions.name: _atom
functions.description: Converts a single token string into an atomic AST value. Handles booleans (#t/#f), numbers (int/float), quoted strings, and symbols.
postconditions: Returns int for numeric strings without '.', float for numeric strings with '.'
Returns unquoted content for strings starting and ending with '"', with '\"' replaced by '"'
Returns the token as-is (as str) for all other cases (symbols)

functions.name: parse
functions.description: Parse an SPL S-expression string into an AST. Enforces a maximum policy size of 64KB (65536 bytes) and validates syntax.

inputs.name: src
inputs.description: SPL S-expression source code to parse

error_data.message: policy exceeds maximum size of 65536 bytes

error_cases.name: empty_source
error_cases.condition: No tokens after tokenizing and stripping

error_cases.name: extra_tokens
error_cases.condition: Tokens remain after parsing complete expression

preconditions: Source must not exceed 65536 bytes when UTF-8 encoded
Source must contain at least one token after stripping whitespace
postconditions: Returns a fully parsed AST representing the S-expression
All tokens are consumed (no extra tokens remain)
Nested lists are represented as Python lists

invariants: MAX_POLICY_BYTES = 65536 - Maximum allowed size for policy source in bytes
Tokenization preserves string literals as complete tokens including quotes
Parentheses '(' and ')' are always separate tokens
Boolean literals '#t' and '#f' are converted to Python True and False
Numeric tokens are converted to int or float based on presence of '.'
Quoted strings have their quotes removed and escape sequences processed
All other tokens are treated as symbols (returned as strings)

data_access.rationale: Pure parsing module with no data persistence or external I/O

authority.rationale: Parser module does not own any data domains, only processes input
